module_mood_contract: learning through contrast — 'I can feel what packaging adds now; I couldn't have read it as a lecture'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named and honoured per beat.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Single-phase lecture. Trace mood_score: 9/10. The recognition arc (you built it → now it has a name) is structurally intact. Opening paragraph ('Pause for a second. You walked into M5 with an un-packaged run…') anchors directly in felt contrast before naming anything. Ronacher three-pattern earns its name from the M4 failure modes stated first. Bridge closes open rather than resolved — M6 is foreshadowed, not delivered. Mood contract 'learning through contrast' lands.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood-floor applies to teaching beats only — setup beats exempt.
rules_evaluated.evidence: File has no setup/launch beats. Every section is a teaching beat (naming a pattern, attributing a practitioner, bridging forward). All score at trace mood 9/10. No beats below 7.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers, not openers.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Strategy doc confirms this lecture is the 'Closing lecture' placed in the module's '## Closing' section after Debrief. It names a pattern the student just lived through (Ronacher's three-pattern from felt evidence). Placement is correct closer.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Minimal lecturing before contrast-mood exercises.
rules_evaluated.evidence: This is the closing lecture — it sits AFTER the exercise, not before. Rule 2 governs pre-exercise dosage. Not applicable.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lecture has no fenced prompt blocks. No forcing functions present. Rule scoped to exercises with prompt blocks.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; trainer manages the room outside text.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No authored conversation cues, pause cues, or 'discuss with…' sentences in body. Hard-grep phrase list clean. Body teaches pattern vocabulary and hands off to M6. Trainer manages cohort timing independently.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-phase failure mode + escape hatch — every forcing function names its dominant failure and one recovery move.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lecture has no phases with forcing functions. Rule applies to exercises. Not applicable.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Teaching moment lands: the named teaching moment reliably triggers across reasonable persona-skill variation.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Teaching moment: the three-pattern earns its name from the failure modes the student just diagnosed in their own M4 run, not from a slide. Opening paragraph activates the return ('You walked into M5 with an un-packaged run that underdelivered. You read it through three lenses…') before naming anything. Trace persona maps each piece to what they built without prompting. Maintainer watch-for confirms: 'The three-pattern earns its name HERE, not in the pre-read. If a sim shows the closing landing as repetition rather than naming, the pre-read leaked.' No pre-read leak detected — learning-through-contrast.md deliberately withholds Ronacher names. Teaching moment structurally reliable.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood doesn't resolve early: this module doesn't preempt the next module's teaching moment.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Bridge to M6 ('Your verifier becomes a judge in the team kit. The kit grows by accretion… That's M6.') foreshadows without delivering. Lecture explicitly states 'M6 asks: do all our runs pass, do they pass faster, who reviewed what?' — names the M6 question without answering it. M5 mood (learning through contrast) resolves appropriately; M6 practitioner-fluency mood is not preempted.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Big Idea fidelity: file leads with or honours the module's Big Idea and mood contract.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Strategy doc M5 Big Idea: 'Learn from the test, re-send packaged.' Lecture opens by grounding in the test-and-return ('You walked into M5 with an un-packaged run that underdelivered… Now the names.'). The naming move is the Big Idea operationalised — packaging earns its vocabulary from felt evidence, not pre-loaded definition. Fidelity intact.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Module-to-module arc: this file's close hands off to the next module's open.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Bridge to M6 section explicitly: 'The verifier is an eval… M6 is where this move scales… Next you will measure the reliability of the verifier you just built.' Strategy doc confirms M6 opens with the second return and diff against the baseline. Handoff is explicit and correctly scoped.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lecture placement (check_lectures.md): meta-frame lectures are closers.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Already evaluated above under check_lectures.md rule 1. Confirmed closer placement.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Epistemic mood ≠ punitive mood.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No punitive framing. The un-packaged run 'underdelivered' — descriptive, not shameful. Counter-camp section ends with 'Two camps, both real… Worth knowing it exists.' Tone is epistemic throughout.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with the discipline, not the failure mode.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Each section leads with the discipline: 'Reference artefact.', 'plan.md the agent owns and mutates.', 'External verifier.', 'Hooks always fire.' Failure modes are stated as the problem each piece solves, not as the headline.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategy-fidelity check — no front-running of downstream teaching moments.
rules_evaluated.evidence: M6 teaching moment (encoding what two runs taught; full practitioner fluency) is not pre-empted. Bridge names the move at high level; M6 owns the operational landing. Evals are named as a bridge concept, not taught operationally here.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Hard-grep phrase list — no room-share commands.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Scanned body: 'tell the person', 'sitting next to you', 'share with your neighbor', 'tell the room', 'say X out loud', 'name X in chat', 'call it out', 'read aloud', 'say it aloud', 'sit with', 'take a moment', 'let it land', 'pause and reflect', 'breathe' — none present.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No em-dashes in body.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Body contains no em-dashes. A colon-delimited structure is used throughout ('In Ronacher's MiniJinja port, the original Rust snapshot tests played this role.'). Parenthetical usage is appropriate (true clarifications).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No narrating the student's past or present state.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Opening uses past-tense recap ('You walked into M5… You read it through three lenses…'). This is a lecture's earned recognition opener — it describes the student's arc to anchor the naming move, not a pat-on-the-head closer. Strategy doc confirms this is load-bearing: 'the naming move lands as recognition, not lecture.' Sutherland-flip not needed here; this is the epistemic move, not state-narration. Carve-out: 'You just felt the shape of it' (line 47) borders on narrating present state, but it's earning the 80/20 claim, not patronising. Acceptable in context.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: LLM-vs-agent vocabulary discipline.
rules_evaluated.evidence: File uses 'agent' for acting (files written, execution), 'LLM' for thinking/drift ('the LLM is forgetful. Drift, half-remembered rules…'), and 'Claude Code' for the product. Discipline is clean.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Source verification TODO items are present in maintainer block — not a story-class blocker but noted.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block flags several URL/number verifications as MUST DO before first cohort. These are technical-class concerns (platform and research claims), not story-class. Story judge records them as observed but does not block on them. Technical-class judge owns verification.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Hooks always fire section (added 2026-05-15) — mood integration check.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The 'Hooks always fire' section (lines 34-38) is a teaching beat added 2026-05-15. Mood score for this beat is consistent with the overall lecture mood (9/10). The passage leads with the discipline ('A hook… fires on a named event… The runtime fires the script; the agent has no say'), then gives the practitioner convention ('Must happen → hook. Recommended → prompt or rule'), then states the cost-benefit ('Bought at the cost of flexibility, given to the work that demands certainty'). The beat earns its place between the three verifier shapes and the Intercom scale section — it generalises the deterministic shell hook the student chose. No mood disruption. Hook event names (SessionStart, UserPromptSubmit, PreToolUse, PostToolUse, Stop) appear; maintainer notes these are live-verified. Story judge does not re-verify platform claims — technical judge's domain.
